interpretation: The inclusion unit is a complete clean 5-minute market; model-ready coverage now has 849 such markets. Full-day continuity is preferred context, not a hard gate: 0 full 288-window days and 3 days with at least 50% of expected windows; the best day has 259/288 windows. 668 feature rows used source-tagged backup BTC prices. The maker 1c proxy has 30 positive coarse rules, but none pass the day-stability gate, so they remain fragile fill-proxy diagnostics. The coarse taker-rule search has 38 pooled holdout-positive rules and 7 day-stable rules. Treat them as candidates for deeper latency-cost modeling, not live edges. With at most one taker entry per market, 73 rule variants are holdout-positive and 36 pass the day-stability gate; the strongest variants still only span 3 test days, so they need more clean-market coverage before sizing. Latency stress keeps 7/7 day-stable taker rules alive at 2s latency with a 2c slippage cap, but 0/7 survive at 10s with the same cap.
